feedback.details: Correctly implements .app-layout with display:flex and background:#0a0a0f. Sidebar is .sidebar-container at exactly 260px width with background:#111827 and flex-shrink:0. Main area uses flex:1 and min-width:0 to prevent overflow. Mobile breakpoint at 767px hides sidebar via translateX(-260px) and shows hamburger button. overflow:hidden on root container prevents horizontal overflow. Both color values and pixel width match spec exactly.

App.tsx has BrowserRouter with '/' → LandingPage and '/c/:conversationId' → ConversationPage. The '/' route renders AppLayout with conversationId=undefined, which shows a proper EmptyState component with instructions and sample prompts. No blank white screen, no expected JS errors. The contract permits this empty-landing approach as an alternative to redirect. Minor note: new conversations are not auto-created on landing.

useMessages hook fetches GET /api/conversations/{id} and sets messages on success. For invalid IDs, the backend returns HTTP 404 with {detail:'Conversation not found'}, the api.ts request() function throws 'API error 404: ...' which the hook catches and sets as error state. ChatArea renders ErrorState with the error message and a Retry button — no crash, no blank screen. Local network fetch should complete well under 2 seconds.

list_conversations() in repository.py uses ORDER BY updated_at DESC, satisfying newest-first sorting. Each ConvItem renders title (14px, fontWeight:500), a relative timestamp via formatRelativeTime() returning formats like 'Xm ago'/'Xh ago'/'Xd ago', and a preview truncated at 77 chars + '…' (total ≤ 80). Empty state renders 'No conversations yet' text with an icon. All criteria met cleanly.

During loading=true (set before fetch, cleared in finally), 4 SkeletonRow components are rendered. Each SkeletonRow contains 3 stacked divs with className='skeleton'. The .skeleton CSS class applies a linear-gradient with 200% background-size animated via 'shimmer' keyframes at 1.5s linear infinite — a proper shimmer effect. No spinner element anywhere in the sidebar. Exceeds the 3-row minimum.

ConvItem applies borderLeft: isActive ? '3px solid #3b82f6' : '3px solid transparent' and background: isActive ? '#1e293b' : 'transparent'. The activeConversationId flows from useParams → ConversationPage → AppLayout → Sidebar → ConvItem, so switching routes (via navigate()) immediately updates the highlight through React's prop propagation. The 3px blue border matches #3b82f6 exactly, and the active background (#1e293b) is distinctly lighter than transparent.

Hovering a ConvItem sets hovered=true and shows the trash SVG button. Clicking fires onDeleteRequest(id) which sets confirmId, opening a ConfirmDialog modal. The modal has Cancel and Delete buttons. On confirm, calls deleteConversation() (DELETE /api/conversations/{id}). On success (204 or ok), closes dialog and calls refetch() — item removed without page reload. On non-2xx failure, setDeleteError(true) displays 'Failed to delete. Please try again.' inside the modal and item remains. The check `if (!res.ok && res.status !== 204)` correctly identifies success (204 has no body, so res.ok is true for 204).

The New Chat button is full-width, background #3b82f6, pinned at the top of the sidebar with padding 12px. Clicking calls createConversation() → POST /api/conversations → 201 response. On success: refetch() updates list, navigate(`/c/${conv.id}`) changes route, onClose() closes mobile sidebar. On error: setNewChatError('Could not create conversation. Please try again.') shown below the button in red (#ef4444). Route does not change on error.

User bubbles: justifyContent:'flex-end', background:'#2563eb', color:'#f1f5f9', maxWidth:'70%'. Assistant bubbles: justifyContent:'flex-start', background:'#1e293b', color:'#f1f5f9', maxWidth:'80%'. Both color values and alignment match spec exactly. However, border-radius deviates: user uses '18px 18px 4px 18px' and assistant uses '18px 18px 18px 4px' — creating a chat-bubble 'tail' effect. The spec says 'rounded-2xl corners' (all corners, ~16px). While 18px is close to rounded-2xl, the 4px corner on each bubble is a notable deviation from uniformly rounded corners. Functional distinction is clear, spec compliance is partial.

SkeletonMessages renders 4 rows with alternating alignment: flex-end (simulating user messages), flex-start (simulating assistant), flex-end, flex-start. Each row has multiple skeleton lines at varying widths. Uses the same .skeleton shimmer class as the sidebar. showSkeleton = loading && !showEmpty, so skeletons appear only when a conversation is loading. Replaced by actual messages once fetch resolves. Exceeds 3-row minimum and correctly alternates alignment.

MarkdownRenderer.tsx uses ReactMarkdown with remarkPlugins={[remarkGfm]}. The component is wrapped in <div className='assistant-content'>. globals.css defines .assistant-content prose styles: paragraphs, ul/ol with padding-left:1.5em, li elements, bold (font-weight:600), em (font-style:italic), inline code with background. remark-gfm enables GFM tables, strikethrough, task lists. Raw markdown syntax like '**bold**' will render as bold text, not asterisks.

CodeBlock uses Prism SyntaxHighlighter with oneDark theme. customStyle sets background:'#0d1117'. The header div (.code-block-header) contains a .code-lang-label span (lowercase) and a CopyButton. Language detection: /language-(\w+)/.exec(className||'') from react-markdown's code component. When language is detected, SyntaxHighlighter renders with that language. When no language, falls back to a <pre> with background:#0d1117 and language label 'plaintext' — copy button still present. Block detection uses isBlock = Boolean(match) || codeStr.includes('\n').

useEffect triggers scrollToBottom when messages.length or streamingContent changes, but only if autoScrollRef.current is true. handleScroll computes distFromBottom = scrollHeight - scrollTop - clientHeight; sets autoScrollRef.current = distFromBottom < 100. When user scrolls up > 100px from bottom, auto-scroll is suppressed. Scrolls re-enable when user scrolls back within 100px. After loading, a 50ms setTimeout calls scrollToBottom('instant') to snap to bottom. Implementation matches spec's 100px threshold exactly.

textarea starts with rows={1} and lineHeight:'24px' giving ~24px initial height. onInput triggers adjustHeight(): sets height:'auto' then Math.min(scrollHeight, 144)px. 144px = 6 × 24px matches ~6 lines max. overflowY set to 'auto' when scrollHeight > 144px, 'hidden' otherwise. After send (handleSend), el.style.height='auto' and el.style.overflowY='hidden' resets to single line. Fully meets spec.

ChatInput uses React state: focused=true onFocus, false onBlur. Both the wrapper div and the textarea element have inline style boxShadow: focused && !isDisabled ? '0 0 0 2px #3b82f6' : 'none'. The textarea itself (with outline:'none') gets the exact box-shadow specified. When not focused, boxShadow is 'none'. Note: globals.css defines .chat-input-wrapper:focus-within but the wrapper div has no className applied — it's purely driven by React state. This works correctly since state-based focus tracking matches focus behavior.

A position:absolute div at bottom:0, height:120px applies background:'linear-gradient(to bottom, transparent, #0a0a0f)' with pointerEvents:'none' and zIndex:1. The chat input wrapper is position:absolute at bottom:0 with zIndex:2, sitting above the gradient. The message scroll container has paddingBottom:140px, giving 140px of space below the last message to prevent it being hidden by the gradient/input. Gradient direction (transparent→opaque) is correct for a fade-in-from-top effect above the input.

handleKeyDown: if e.key==='Enter' && !e.shiftKey → e.preventDefault() then handleSend(). Shift+Enter has no special handling so textarea default (insert newline) applies. handleSend: const content = el.value.trim(); if (!content || isDisabled) return — empty/whitespace-only input fails the falsy check and returns without calling onSend or any API. The guard is correct. Pressing Enter on empty input produces no network activity.

isDisabled = disabled || isStreaming. textarea: disabled={isDisabled}, cursor:'not-allowed' when disabled. Send button: disabled={isDisabled}, cursor:'not-allowed' when disabled. Wrapper div: opacity:0.7 when isDisabled. handleSend: if (!content || isDisabled) return — blocks even if onClick fires somehow. handleKeyDown calls handleSend which also guards on isDisabled. isStreaming is set in useStreamingResponse via setIsStreaming(true) at start and setIsStreaming(false) in finally block after stream ends. No double-submission possible.

index.html loads Inter (400,500,600,700) and JetBrains Mono via Google Fonts with preconnect hints. globals.css sets font-family:'Inter', -apple-system, ... and color:var(--text-primary) (#f1f5f9) on html/body/#root. Message content inherits #f1f5f9. Sidebar timestamps use color:'#94a3b8' (--text-secondary) exactly. Sidebar branding and preview text use #475569 (--text-tertiary) not secondary — minor inconsistency with spec saying labels use secondary. Overall palette is applied consistently and Inter will resolve as the first font when loaded from Google Fonts.

overallSummary: Sprint 4 passes all 19 criteria at or above the 7-point threshold. The implementation is thorough and well-structured: a clean two-column layout with proper CSS variables, React Router setup for both routes, a fully-functional sidebar with skeleton loaders, active state, delete-with-confirmation dialog, and new chat creation. Message rendering uses react-markdown with remark-gfm and react-syntax-highlighter (oneDark) with language labels and copy buttons. The chat input auto-expands, glows on focus, and correctly disables during streaming. The only notable deviation from spec is the chat bubble border-radius using a 'tail' style (18px/4px) instead of uniformly rounded-2xl corners, and the landing page showing an empty state instead of redirecting to a new conversation route — both of which the contract explicitly permits. Code quality is clean with proper separation of concerns across hooks, components, and API layer.
